data.title: TCLE (01070.HK) saw a 62.0% YoY surge in H1 2025 profits. Mid-to-High-End Strategy driving quality global growth.
data.description: TCL Electronics has seen significant success in its global business operations by focusing on a mid-to-high-end strategy. By prioritizing quality growth and expanding its global reach, the company's core business has experienced tangible results. This approach has led to an increase in revenue and market presence, with key figures demonstrating the effectiveness of their strategy. This growth highlights the success of TCL Electronics' emphasis on mid-to-high-end products and global expansion efforts.

data.title: ASUS announces Back to School Sale on August 22–September 4, offering up to $700 off and savings of up to 46%.
data.description: ASUS has revealed a Back to School Sale starting from August 22 to September 4, featuring discounts of up to $700 and maximum savings reaching up to 46%.

data.title: Cryptocurrency market down, traders say it's a healthy retest. Bitcoin at $112,611, Ethereum at $4,305, XRP at $2.85, Dogecoin at $0.2153. Liquidated traders at $240.55 million in 24 hours. Bitcoin ETFs had $194 million outflows, while Ethereum ETFs saw $287 million inflows. Top gainers include OKB, Morpho, Monero. Ethereum consolidates below $4,300. Bitcoin expected to rise in September to $113,000.
data.description: Cryptocurrency markets are experiencing a slight downturn on Friday morning ahead of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's speech at Jackson Hole. The current prices are as follows: Bitcoin at $112,611, Ethereum at $4,305, Solana at $181.72, XRP at $2.85, Dogecoin at $0.2153, and Shiba Inu at $0.00001234. In the past 24 hours, 81,748 traders were liquidated, resulting in losses amounting to $240.55 million. Data from SoSoValue indicates net outflows of $194 million from spot Bitcoin ETFs on Wednesday, while spot Ethereum ETFs saw net inflows of $287 million during the same period. Top gainers in the past day include OKB, Morpho, and Monero. Ethereum is consolidating below the $4,300 mark, prompting speculation about the possibility of reaching either $5,000 or $4,000 next. Anthony Pompliano predicts that Bitcoin will "come back alive again" in September, with a projected value of $113,000. For more details, visit the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: SBI inks deals with Circle, Ripple, and Startale for stablecoin ventures and asset trading in Japan.
data.description: SBI has joined forces with Circle, Ripple, and Startale to introduce stablecoin projects and a platform for trading tokenized assets in Japan. This collaboration aims to revolutionize the digital currency market and enhance financial services. The partnership involves key players in the industry and promises to bring about significant advancements. Stay tuned for more updates on this exciting venture.

data.description: This week, notable tech updates surfaced as per reports from PR Newswire. Google unveiled a significant energy collaboration, while IBM and NASA introduced solar weather technology. Additionally, a partnership called NightDragon was established to enhance national defense initiatives. The announcements were made in New York on August 22, 2025, attracting attention from thousands of media outlets.

data.title: AI in food safety and quality control market growing at 30.9% CAGR, driven by demand for predictive analytics and automated inspections.
data.description: The adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) is increasing in the global food safety and quality control sector due to a rising demand for predictive analytics, automated inspections, and end-to-end traceability. A study by BCC Research revealed that in 2025, there has been a significant surge in the use of AI in this industry. This trend is driven by the need to enhance food safety measures and maintain quality standards. The report highlights the growing reliance on AI technologies to streamline processes and ensure compliance with regulations. As of August 22, 2025, AI is being embraced by food safety and quality control professionals worldwide, showcasing the industry's evolution towards more advanced and efficient practices.

data.title: Global 3D Printing Filament market projected to reach $4.20 billion by 2030 per MarketsandMarkets™ report.
data.description: A recent report released on August 22, 2025, in DELRAY BEACH, Florida, highlights the significant growth projected in the 3D Printing Filament Market. The report focuses on various types of filaments including plastics, metals, ceramics, and others, categorizing the market based on end-use industries such as Aerospace & Defense, Medical & Dental, Automotive, Consumer Goods, and others. The forecast indicates a promising outlook for the market globally up to 2030. The 3D Printing Filament Market is poised for substantial growth, encompassing a wide range of industries and materials.

data.title: Prosafe SE commenced a subscription period for an offering of up to 17,868,651 warrants, board members have until Dec 31st to subscribe.
data.description: On 22 August 2025, Prosafe SE referred back to the stock exchange announcement from 23 July 2025 about initiating the Subscription Period for the Warrants Offering. The offering consists of up to 17,868,651 warrants. If individuals related to the Company’s board of directors or management are unable to subscribe during the Subscription Period due to securities regulations, they can do so within two weeks after these restrictions end. The resolution to offer the Warrants was made during the Company's extraordinary general meeting on 16 May 2025. All exercises must be completed by 31 December 2025.
Latest announcement regarding Nykredit Realkredit A/S drawings (CK95) has been released.

data.title: US House Republicans sneak CBDC ban into defense bill, highlighting America's unique position as global CBDC adoption rises.
data.description: House Republicans have introduced a new provision emphasizing America's unique position as global adoption of Central Bank Digital Currencies (CBDCs) continues to rise. This move underscores the importance of examining the rapid growth of CBDCs worldwide and the United States' divergent approach in this evolving financial landscape. The provision serves as a reminder of the escalating adoption of CBDCs globally and the necessity for America to consider its strategic position in this growing trend.

data.title: Denmark aims to boost children's reading habits. Plans to remove 25% VAT on books in response to the "reading crisis."
data.description: In response to a severe "reading crisis" in the country, the Danish government has proposed to eliminate the 25 percent value-added tax on books. This move aims to make books more accessible and affordable for the population. The initiative comes at a crucial time as the nation grapples with declining reading rates. By exempting books from this tax, the government hopes to encourage more people to read, with the ultimate goal of improving literacy levels. This decision highlights the government's commitment to addressing the challenges faced in promoting reading and literacy, with the potential to positively impact the population's reading habits.
